title: Where did I learn that wanting was wrong?
subtitle: A journal set for tracing the origin of guilt around wanting wealth.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for Week 3 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for tracing the specific message, person, or moment that taught you wanting money was selfish, excessive, or not for you.
sourceNotes: Drawn from Day 15 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 3 of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the guilt around wanting money is present but you don't know where it came from.

content.text: A journal set for Day 15 of the MONEY program: releasing the guilt around wanting wealth by finding where it started. Five prompts, around thirty minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What do I actually want, financially — if I stop editing it?
Write the full version. Not the acceptable version. What you actually want.
What feeling comes up when I write that?
Name the feeling precisely. Guilt, embarrassment, fear of judgement, something else.
Where did I learn that wanting was wrong?
A person, a comment, a repeated message. Trace it as specifically as you can.
What blessings in my life remind me that having more lets me give more?
Name two or three specific examples — times when having resources let you help, give, or create something.
What would change if wanting was simply allowed?
In how you talk about money, how you set prices, how you ask. Write the version of you who doesn't apologise for wanting.
Drawn from Day 15 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 3 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
